subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Your Body's Emergency Protocol
description: Learn Your Body's Emergency Protocol in one minute.

hook.text: You cut your finger. Within seconds, your body launches a multi-phase rescue mission.

buildup.text: Healing isn't one process—it's a cascade of overlapping phases that can last weeks.

discovery.text: Phase one: hemostasis stops the bleeding. Phase two: inflammation cleans the wound.

twist.text: Phase three rebuilds tissue. Phase four remodels it—and can continue for over a year.

climax.text: Every phase must complete in order. Disrupting one delays the entire chain.

punchline.text: Your body runs a four-phase construction project for every cut.

quiz.question: What is the first phase of wound healing?
